Status

This policy is still a draft and has not been sent to staff.

Publishing locks in a version number. If you re-publish after edits, the version increments and prior acknowledgements no longer count, staff are asked to re-acknowledge.

Acceptable Use of AI Tools at [Company Name]
Effective: 18 Jul 2026 · Version: 1.0 · Owner: [Role, e.g. DPO / CISO / Head of IT] · Review cycle: Quarterly
1. Purpose

This policy describes how staff at [Company Name] may use generative AI tools (such as ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, or any other AI assistant) in the course of their work, setting out what is encouraged, what is permitted with conditions, and what is forbidden.

2. Scope

Applies to all employees, contractors, interns and consultants performing work for [Company Name], on company-owned and personal devices used for work, covering all AI tools whether procured by the company or used personally.

3. Approved tools
ChatGPT Enterprise (EU region)
Microsoft 365 Copilot

Other tools require explicit approval from [Role, e.g. DPO / CISO / Head of IT] before use. Free or personal-tier consumer AI tools are not approved for the data categories in Section 5.

4. Permitted uses

Drafting and editing work with human review; summarising appropriate material; translation and rephrasing; brainstorming and structuring thinking; explaining concepts; technical assistance on appropriate data.

5. Forbidden inputs

Customer or contact personal data; special-category personal data; third-party contracts or legal drafts; source code we don't own or licence; non-public financial data; authentication secrets; documents classified Confidential or higher; HR records; anything held under a duty of confidence — must never be entered into a free or personal-tier AI tool.

6. Output handling

AI-generated content must be reviewed by a human before use. Claims affecting a decision must be independently verified. AI involvement in customer-facing material must be disclosed per EU AI Act Article 50. No decision affecting someone's livelihood or legal position may be made on AI output alone.

7. Logging

Significant AI-assisted work is logged in: Shared AI Use Register (spreadsheet). "Significant" means the use affected another person, represented the company externally, touched sensitive data, or supported an irreversible/expensive decision.

8. Settings and configuration

Training-data opt-out enabled wherever offered on a work account; retention follows the company's procurement contract; MFA is required on all work AI accounts.

9. Vendor changes

Material changes to an approved tool's terms, data handling, sub-processors or model must be reviewed by [DPO Name] before continued use.

10. Incidents

Report within the hour to security@example.com: data pasted into the wrong tool, harmful AI output, an AI-powered scam, or a vendor security incident. Do not delete the evidence.

11. Training

All staff complete the AI Safe@Work course within thirty days of joining and refresh annually.

12. Roles and responsibilities

Every member of staff complies with t

Approve new AI tool for use					
Review vendor DPA and contract terms					
Conduct vendor security assessment					
Assess GDPR / EU AI Act compliance fit					
Run DPIA on new tool use case

Maintain AI Tool Register

Track AI training completion					
Respond to AI incident (investigation)					
Notify regulator of AI-related breach					
Quarterly governance review (steering committee)					
Report to CEO on governance maturity					

R = Responsible (does the work) · A = Accountable (final say) · C = Consulted (gives input) · I = Informed (told the outcome).

AI Steering Group — Terms of Reference
1. Purpose

The AI Steering Group is the body accountable for the safe, lawful and effective use of artificial intelligence across [Organisation]. It owns the AI governance framework, approves AI use cases, oversees risk, and reports to the board.

2. Scope

All AI and generative-AI tools used by staff; all use cases touching personal data, customers, employment decisions or regulated activity; the policies, registers and assessments this platform tracks. Out of scope: day-to-day IT helpdesk support and individual HR matters.

3. Membership
ROLE	HELD BY
Chair	[Senior owner, e.g. COO / CISO]
Data protection / compliance	[DPO name]
IT / security	[Name / role]
Procurement / legal	[Name / role]
Business / operations	[Name / role]
Executive sponsor	[Name / role]
4. Responsibilities

Own the AI governance framework and AUP; approve, reject or condition AI use cases; oversee the registers; set and monitor training; review incidents and notification decisions; track EU AI Act and ISO/IEC 42001 obligations; report governance maturity quarterly.

5. Meetings, quorum and decisions

Frequency: Quarterly, plus on demand for urgent approvals. Quorum: the Chair plus at least three members, including data protection. Decisions: by consensus where possible, otherwise majority with the Chair holding the casting vote. Minutes: circulated within five working days.

6. Reporting

The Chair reports to the board each quarter on use cases approved/rejected, open high risks, training completion, incidents, and progress against EU AI Act and ISO/IEC 42001 obligations.

How "send to staff" works here

Share this artifact's link with your team (use the share / publish option above the conversation). Everyone who opens it sees the same live data. Add people to the roster below, then use Copy announcement message or the per-person Email button to notify them — each person opens the link, finds their name under "Acknowledge the policy", and signs off. Their acknowledgement appears here instantly for everyone.
